capabilities: Implement secure secret storage by encrypting secrets at rest and enforcing strict access control policies to prevent unauthorized access.

procedure.action: Assert input secrets by ensuring they conform to expected formats and lengths using JSON Schema validation; log validation errors with specific field paths.
Encrypt secrets using AES-256-GCM encryption with a unique key per secret; store encryption keys securely using a hardware security module (HSM).

Store encrypted secrets in a dedicated secrets management service like HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager; ensure service access logs are enabled using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Execute strict access control by configuring role-based access policies in the secrets management service; audit access logs for unauthorized attempts by executing the transformation function on each input record; assert output_schema_valid = true and error_count < 1; log transformation_duration_ms.

Rotate encryption keys periodically by generating new keys and re-encrypting stored secrets; verify successful re-encryption by comparing hash values using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure for securely storing secrets by encrypting them at rest and applying strict access control measures.
title: Secure Secret Storage Implementation

failureConditions: Unauthorized access occurs when access control policies are misconfigured, causing sensitive data exposure to unauthorized users.
Data corruption happens when encryption keys are mishandled or lost, causing inability to decrypt stored secrets.
Security breaches occur when secrets are stored without encryption, allowing attackers to read plaintext data if storage is compromised.
metrics: input secrets by ensuring they conform to expected formats and lengths using JSON Schema validation
